---
title: "Dr. Gang He"
#description-meta: "{{< meta description-meta >}}"
image: files/gang-he-profile.jpeg
page-navigation: false
toc: false
#sidebar: false
#page-layout: article

about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 80%
  links:
    - icon: sun
      text: Mainpage
      href: https://www.ganghe.net
    - icon: globe
      text: CUNYpage
      href: https://marxe.baruch.cuny.edu/about/faculty-and-staff/full-time-faculty/
    - icon: envelope
      text: E-mail
      href: mailto:gang.he@baruch.cuny.edu
#    - icon: github
#      text: Github
#      href: https://github.com/drganghe
#    - icon: twitter
#      text: Twitter
#      href: https://twitter.com/DrGangHe
#    - icon: mastodon
#      text: Mastodon
#      href: https://mastodon.social/@hegang      
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/hegang
    - icon: lightbulb
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8416-1965  
    - icon: archive
      text: Scopus
      href: https://www.scopus.com/authid/detail.uri?authorId=55607981900
    - icon: google
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=vf90AuEAAAAJ
    - icon: file-earmark-pdf
      text: Research Gate
      href: https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Gang-He-19

listing:
  - id: recent-posts
    contents: /posts
    type: grid
    max-items: 3
    page-size: 0 #set as 0 will remove the page setting
    fields: [image, date, title, author, description]
    max-description-length: 75
    sort: 
      - "date desc"
      - "title asc"
---

<link rel="me" href="https://mastodon.social/@hegang">


:::{#hero-heading}

Dr. He is an energy scholar, researcher, and teacher.  He is an assistant professor in the [Marxe School of Public and International Affairs](https://marxe.baruch.cuny.edu/homepage/fall-2023-new-faculty/), Baruch College, City University of New York. He studies energy systems, energy and climate policy, and clean energy transition.

Dr. He's work has been published in high-impact interdisciplinary and field journals such as [*Nature*](/posts/2022-10-nature-cost-savings-of-global-solar-pv-value-chains/index.qmd), [*Nature Energy*](/posts/2023-07-nenergy-climate-change-impacts-supply-demand-match/index.qmd), [*Nature Water*](/posts/2023-08-naturewater-global-assessment-of-the-carbon-water-tradeoff-of-dry-cooling/index.qmd), [*Nature Communications*](/posts/2020-05-ncomms-rapid-re-cost/index.qmd), [*One Earth*](/posts/2020-08-oneearth-coal-just-transition/index.qmd), [*Environmental Science \& Technology*](/posts/2016-est-switch-china/index.qmd), [*Energy Policy*](/posts/2013-ep-carbon-offsetters-paradox/index.qmd). His research was reported by [*Nature*](/posts/2020-10-nature-news-carbon-neutrality/index.qmd), *Scientific American*, *Carbon Brief*, *National Geographic*, *New York Times*, *E\&E News*, among others. 

Dr. He’s work has direct policy impacts. He has testified for the [New York State Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act](/posts/2019-nyclcpa-testimony/index.qmd) before it became law and has advised New York State Climate Action Council’s [Scoping Plan](/posts/2021-12-nysclcpa-scoping-plan-draft/index.qmd). He has also involved in the U.S.-China collaboration on energy and climate change. 

:::

Dr. He’s most recent collaborative analysis on the cost savings of global solar PV supply chains was published by [*Nature*](/posts/2022-10-nature-cost-savings-of-global-solar-pv-value-chains/index.qmd), and was awarded the [Best Poster Award](/posts/2022-12-iamc-2022-best-poster-award/index.qmd) by the Integrated Assessment Modeling Consortium (2022). He was selected as Youth Delegate to UNFCCC CoP11 (2005), Asia 21 Young Leaders (2007), Aspen Environment Forum Scholars (2011), Young Scholar of Institute for New Economic Thinking (2013), and [ITIF Energy Innovation Policy and Management Scholar](https://itif.org/events/2019/05/19/2019-itif-energy-innovation-boot-camp-for-early-career-scholars/) (2019). 

Dr. He has worked for the [Department of Technology and Society](https://www.stonybrook.edu/experts/profile/gang-he) at Stony Brook University, the [Program on Energy and Sustainable Development](https://pesd.fsi.stanford.edu/people/Gang_He) at Stanford University. He received his Ph.D. in [Energy and Resources](https://erg.berkeley.edu/people/gang-he/) from University of California, Berkeley. He also holds an M.A. in Climate and Society from Columbia University, and a B.S. and M.S. in Geography from Peking University.

## Recent Posts
Check out the latest &nbsp;[Papers](posts.qmd#category=paper)&nbsp;, &nbsp;[News](posts.qmd#category=news)&nbsp;, &nbsp;[Events](posts.qmd#category=event)&nbsp;, and &nbsp;[More »](/posts.qmd)

:::{#recent-posts}
:::
